_comment: Hand-curated (question, SQL) pairs with paraphrase variants and provenance. When a new question's embedding cosine-matches a verified question (or any of its QuestionVariants) above threshold (default 0.90), the system uses the SQL below directly and skips the LLM. Domain-agnostic â€” works for any schema. Safe to leave empty (feature inactive).

_format.question: the canonical phrasing of the question
_format.questionVariants: list of paraphrases â€” each is embedded separately so the same SQL covers many natural phrasings without a duplicate entry
_format.sql: the SQL that answers it correctly. Use the same parameter style as the rest of the system (literals are fine â€” no @p params in verified queries).
_format.shape: canonical shape: SEL / FLT / CNT / AGG / GRP / TOP / JOI / ANT / PER / MAX / MIN / DIS / REF / TRD / PCT / COH / ANM / FUN / CUM
_format.verifiedAt: ISO date when this entry was blessed
_format.verifiedBy: person / role who verified this SQL is correct
_format.useAsOnboarding: true if this entry is suitable for the example-questions menu
_format.minSimilarity: optional: override the per-question similarity threshold (0.0-1.0).
_format.tags: optional list of tags for organization.
_format.description: optional human note on why this entry exists.

queries.question: how many tickets do we have in total
questionVariants: give me the total number of tickets
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Canonical total-tickets count â€” covers paraphrases like 'how many tickets', 'count of all tickets', 'total tickets'.

queries.question: show me all open tickets
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Lookup-join example via TicketStatuses.Name â€” exercises the FKâ†’label rewrite path. Arabic variants added 2026-05-18 after F56-AR-data: 'Ø§Ø¹Ø±Ø¶ Ø§Ù„ØªØ°Ø§ÙƒØ± Ø§Ù„Ù…ÙØªÙˆØ­Ø©' spec extractor lost the Ø§Ù„Ù…ÙØªÙˆØ­Ø© (Open) predicate and returned all tickets.

queries.question: ticket count grouped by status
questionVariants: count tickets grouped by status name
break down ticket counts by status
queries.sql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Standard per-dimension breakdown â€” exercises GROUP BY on a lookup-table label.

queries.question: top 5 users by tickets created
questionVariants: who created the most tickets top 5
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 5 u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
queries.description: Top-5 creators. minSimilarity raised 2026-05-19 to prevent absorbing 'top 10' queries (those go to top-10-creators).

queries.question: top 10 users by tickets created
questionVariants: show the top 10 users by number of tickets created
who created the most tickets top 10
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 10 u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
queries.description: Top-10 creators variant. Added 2026-05-19 after RU-UW-01 ('Show the top 10 users by number of tickets created') timed out at 600s â€” fell to LLM because the only TOP-N-creators entry was hardcoded for N=5.

queries.question: users who have created more than 5 tickets
questionVariants: which users have created more than 5 tickets
users that opened more than 5 tickets
users who created more than five tickets
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName HAVING COUNT(t.Id) > 5 ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
queries.description: HAVING COUNT(*) > N on users. Added 2026-05-19 after RU-UW-10 timed out at 600s â€” no VQ existed for the HAVING-on-users pattern.

queries.question: tickets with no comments yet
questionVariants: which tickets have no comments yet
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM TicketComments c WHERE c.TicketId = t.Id) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: NOT EXISTS anti-join: tickets that lack a corresponding TicketComments row. Added 2026-05-19 after RH-HR-005 timed out at 600s â€” no VQ existed for the 'no comments' anti-join pattern.

queries.question: list users with no tickets
questionVariants: users who never created a ticket
users who have not opened any ticket
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: Anti-join shape â€” exercises the 'with no X' refusal-of-INNER-JOIN-default path.

queries.question: tickets created today vs yesterday
questionVariants: compare today and yesterday ticket counts
how many tickets today compared to yesterday
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Today, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(DATEADD(DAY,-1,GETDATE()) AS DATE) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Yesterday FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Period comparison via conditional aggregation. Pattern: SUM(CASE WHEN <bucketA>) + SUM(CASE WHEN <bucketB>). Operators on other schemas: replace Tickets/IsDeleted/CreatedAt with the equivalent fact-table date column and soft-delete flag.

queries.question: compare tickets created this month vs last month
questionVariants: this month versus last month ticket count
tickets this month compared to last month
compare ticket counts between this month and last month
compare this month to last month tickets
tickets created this month versus last month
this month vs last month tickets
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ThisMonth, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(MONTH,-1,DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1)) AND CreatedAt < DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LastMonth FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: MoM ticket-creation comparison. Same pattern as today-vs-yesterday at month granularity.

queries.question: tickets resolved this year vs last year
questionVariants: this year versus last year resolved tickets
year over year resolved ticket count
compare ticket volume this year versus last year
compare this year vs last year ticket volume
compare tickets this year and last year
this year vs last year tickets
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(ResolvedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ThisYear, SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(ResolvedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE())-1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LastYear FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: YoY resolved-ticket comparison. Note the IS NOT NULL guard on ResolvedAt â€” analyst pattern for 'completed' counts.

queries.question: tickets created in 2025 vs 2024
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(CreatedAt) = 2025 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Y2025, SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(CreatedAt) = 2024 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Y2024 FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Explicit-year comparison (no GETDATE relative-math). The hardcoded years are intentional here â€” paraphrases like '2024 vs 2023' will require their own entry or a generic pattern.

queries.question: this quarter vs last quarter ticket count
questionVariants: current quarter compared to previous quarter tickets
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETDATE()), 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ThisQuarter, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETDATE())-1, 0) AND CreatedAt < DATEADD(QUARTER, DATEDIFF(QUARTER, 0, GETDATE()), 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LastQuarter FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: QoQ comparison. Uses DATEDIFF/DATEADD on the QUARTER datepart for quarter-boundary math.

queries.question: tickets created in the last 7 days vs the previous 7 days
questionVariants: last 7 days vs previous 7 days ticket creation
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Last7Days, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-14,GETDATE()) AND CreatedAt < DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Previous7Days FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Rolling-window comparison â€” exercises the WoW shape that BI tools often expose.

queries.question: monthly ticket trend last 6 months
questionVariants: show the monthly ticket trend for the past 6 months
trend of ticket creation by month for half a year
monthly ticket count for the last 6 months
monthly tickets created last half year
queries.sql: SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(MONTH,-6,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month
queries.description: Monthly time-series bucketing â€” the canonical 'show me a trend' answer. minSim lowered + variants expanded 2026-05-19 after C66 LLM-timed-out at 640s â€” the test phrasing 'monthly ticket trend last 6 months' didn't cosine-match strongly enough.

queries.question: daily ticket creation count for the past 14 days
questionVariants: tickets per day for the last 14 days
two weeks of daily ticket counts
daily ticket trend last two weeks
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-14,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Daily time-series bucketing â€” same shape at day grain.

queries.question: ticket counts by year
queries.sql: SELECT YEAR(CreatedAt) AS Year, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY YEAR(CreatedAt) ORDER BY Year
queries.description: Yearly time-series â€” analyst's 'all-time by year' breakdown.

queries.question: what percentage of tickets are critical priority
questionVariants: ratio of critical to all tickets as a percent
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN p.Name = 'Critical' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS PercentCritical FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Percentage of a lookup-value within total â€” SUM(CASE)/NULLIF(COUNT) pattern with lookup-join.

queries.question: resolution rate this month resolved divided by created
questionVariants: monthly resolution rate as a percentage
what percent of this month tickets got resolved
resolved over created ratio this month
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND ResolvedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),0) AS DECIMAL(6,2)) AS ResolutionRatePct FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Operational KPI: resolved-this-month / created-this-month as a percent. Two SUM(CASE WHEN) over NULLIF.

queries.question: what percent of tickets are unassigned
questionVariants: share of tickets without an assignee
percent of tickets with no assignee
how many tickets are unassigned in percentage
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN AssignedToUserId IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS PercentUnassigned FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Percent of rows where a nullable FK is NULL â€” common backlog-health KPI.

queries.question: average resolution time in days grouped by priority
questionVariants: avg time to resolve by priority
how long do tickets take to resolve broken down by priority
queries.sql: SELECT p.Name AS Priority, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionDays FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name
queries.description: Cohort metric: average duration grouped by a lookup dimension. DATEDIFF + AVG + GROUP BY.

queries.question: average ticket count per entity in the last 30 days
questionVariants: entity level ticket volume in the past month
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name AS Entity, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM Entitys e LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0 WHERE e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Name
queries.description: Per-entity ticket count in a recency window. LEFT JOIN preserves zero-ticket entities.

queries.question: median resolution time in days across all resolved tickets
questionVariants: median time to resolve a ticket
what is the median resolution duration
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) OVER () AS MedianResolutionDays FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Median = PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) â€” ONE row (median across all). minSim raised 2026-05-19 to prevent absorbing 'median by priority' queries.

queries.question: cohort median resolution by priority
questionVariants: median days to resolve by priority
median ticket resolution time per priority
median resolution duration per priority cohort
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.Name AS Priority, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) OVER (PARTITION BY p.Name) AS MedianDays FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) PARTITION BY priority â€” returns median per priority. Added 2026-05-19 after C65 fell to AVG-per-priority via cosine miss.

queries.question: which day in the last 30 days had the most tickets created
questionVariants: peak ticket day in the past 30 days
busiest day last month for tickets
highest ticket count day in last 30 days
busiest day of the last 30 days for ticket creation
busiest day of the last 30 days
which day had the most tickets
day with the most tickets created
peak day in last 30 days for ticket creation
most active day for tickets in the last 30 days
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 1 CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Peak-day analysis: TOP 1 over a daily bucket + ORDER BY count desc. minSimilarity lowered + variants expanded 2026-05-18 after F42-ANM-peak fell to spec extractor and emitted a flat COUNT(*) (no GROUP BY).

queries.question: top 3 entities by SLA breach count
questionVariants: which entities breach sla the most top 3
three entities with the highest sla breach count
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 3 e.Name AS Entity, SUM(CASE WHEN t.IsSlaBreached = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS SlaBreachCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 AND e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Name ORDER BY SlaBreachCount DESC
queries.description: Top-N by a flag-driven counter â€” SUM(CASE WHEN flag=1) per dimension, sorted desc.

queries.question: ratio of resolved to open tickets in 2025
questionVariants: resolved over open tickets ratio for 2025
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(1.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN s.Name = 'Resolved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN s.Name = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),0) AS DECIMAL(8,3)) AS ResolvedToOpenRatio FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE YEAR(t.CreatedAt) = 2025 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Funnel KPI: resolved-over-open ratio within a year. Two SUM(CASE) over NULLIF â€” like resolution-rate but scoped to a year.

queries.question: running total of tickets created in the last 30 days by day
questionVariants: cumulative ticket count by day last 30 days
daily running total of tickets for the past month
ticket creation cumulative over the last 30 days
queries.sql: SELECT Day, SUM(DayCount) OVER (ORDER BY Day ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS RunningTotal FROM (SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS DayCount FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE)) d ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Running total via SUM(...) OVER (ORDER BY ... ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING). Wraps a daily-count subquery for clean partitioning.

queries.question: list ticket numbers
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Plain selection of TicketNumber only.

queries.question: show me all user emails
queries.sql: SELECT Email FROM AspNetUsers
queries.description: User email column only. Variants expanded 2026-05-18 after F02-SEL fell to spec extractor (74s, 3 LLM calls).

queries.question: list entity names
queries.sql: SELECT Name FROM Entitys WHERE IsActive = 1
queries.description: Active entity names only â€” IsActive filter included.

queries.question: display each user's username together with their email
questionVariants: show me user names and emails
give me names and emails of users
display username and email for every user
each user with their username and email
list all users with their email addresses
user list with usernames and emails
queries.sql: SELECT UserName, Email FROM AspNetUsers
queries.description: Two-column projection from AspNetUsers. Tightened to 0.92 (was 0.65) on 2026-05-20 â€” the loose threshold was catching unrelated 'users with admin role', 'users with creator email' patterns and returning the unjoined SELECT.

queries.question: tickets created in the last 7 days
questionVariants: tickets opened during the past 7 days
tickets opened in the last 7 days
tickets opened in the past week
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Rolling 7-day filter. Tightened to 0.94 on 2026-05-20 because 'tickets created in the last 30 days' was matching this entry and returning 7-day SQL.

queries.question: tickets created in the last 30 days
questionVariants: tickets created in the past 30 days
tickets opened in the last 30 days
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Rolling 30-day filter. Added in Round 2 of the suite-10 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: tickets that are open and critical
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND p.Name = 'Critical' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Two lookup joins + AND filter on both status and priority.

queries.question: tickets with critical or high priority
questionVariants: tickets that are critical or high
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE p.Name IN ('Critical','High') AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: IN clause on lookup column name.

queries.question: tickets with critical priority
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE p.Name = 'Critical' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Single-priority filter. Tightened to 0.94 on 2026-05-20 because 'tickets with high priority' was matching this and returning Critical SQL.

queries.question: tickets with high priority
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE p.Name = 'High' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: High-priority filter. Added in Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20) to stop the critical-priority entry from over-matching.

queries.question: tickets that have been resolved
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title, ResolvedAt FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: IS NOT NULL filter on ResolvedAt â€” projects the resolved-at column too. Added after R07-FLT-null in regression fell through to LLM (8 retries) and produced a SQL with wrong projection.

queries.question: first 5 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 5 TicketNumber, Title, CreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt ASC
queries.description: TOP N with ORDER BY asc â€” 'oldest' / 'first' synonym handled.

queries.question: latest 10 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 10 TicketNumber, Title, CreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt DESC
queries.description: TOP 10 â€” 'latest' / 'newest' synonym. minSimilarity raised to 0.88 2026-05-18 so it no longer absorbs 'most recent 5' style questions (those go to latest-5-tickets).

queries.question: most recent 5 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 5 TicketNumber, Title, CreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt DESC
queries.description: TOP 5 variant. Added 2026-05-18 after F10-TOP fell to the generic latest-N entry which has TOP 10 hardcoded. Until spec extractor supports a {N} slot, explicit N entries are the safest fix.

queries.question: latest 3 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 3 TicketNumber, Title, CreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt DESC
queries.description: TOP 3 variant. Added alongside latest-5 2026-05-18 for coverage of common small-N requests.

queries.question: latest 20 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 20 TicketNumber, Title, CreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt DESC
queries.description: TOP 20 variant. Added alongside latest-5 2026-05-18.

queries.question: tickets whose title mentions login
questionVariants: tickets with login in the title
tickets that mention login in title
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.Title LIKE '%login%' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Title-only LIKE filter. Added 2026-05-18 after F09-FLT-like â€” spec extractor over-broadened LIKE to 4 text columns (Title, Description, ResolutionSummary, RootCause). Stopgap for the 'login' keyword; proper fix is spec-extractor change to honor explicit 'title' column hint.

queries.question: count of active users
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 1
queries.description: Count of users where IsActive=1.

queries.question: how many active entities
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Entitys WHERE IsActive = 1
queries.description: Counts active entities â€” fixes the LLM confusion of 'entities' with 'users'.

queries.question: how many open tickets
questionVariants: how many open tickets do we have
how many open tickets do we have right now
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: COUNT with single lookup-join filter. Variants expanded 2026-05-18 (combined-suite run) after RE-EZ-002 'How many open tickets do we have right now?' fell to list-tickets path.

queries.question: how many distinct ticket statuses
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Id) AS DistinctStatuses FROM TicketStatuses
queries.description: COUNT DISTINCT â€” pattern the LLM frequently misses. minSim raised 2026-05-19 to prevent absorbing 'distinct ticket statuses' (list) queries â€” those go to list-distinct-statuses.

queries.question: tickets ordered by creation date descending
questionVariants: show all tickets ordered by creation date descending
tickets sorted by creation date desc
tickets in descending order of creation
list tickets ordered by created date desc
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt DESC
queries.description: Plain ORDER BY ... DESC on tickets. Added 2026-05-19 after C72-ORDER-by LLM-timed-out at 723s â€” no VQ existed for explicit ordering patterns.

queries.question: distinct ticket statuses
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT Name FROM TicketStatuses
queries.description: SELECT DISTINCT Name (returns LIST of status names, not count). Added 2026-05-19 after C14-DIS in iter 1: the only existing entry was COUNT DISTINCT which won the cosine race and returned a scalar 7 instead of the 7 status names.

queries.question: how many unique users have created a ticket
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CreatedByUserId) AS DistinctCreators FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: COUNT DISTINCT on an FK column.

queries.question: mean ticket age in days
questionVariants: mean age of tickets in days
queries.sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgAgeDays FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: AVG of DATEDIFF cast to FLOAT â€” model often emits COUNT instead.

queries.question: earliest ticket date
queries.sql: SELECT MIN(CreatedAt) AS EarliestCreatedAt FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: MIN on date column. minSim lowered + variants expanded 2026-05-19 after C17 LLM-timed-out at 648s for 'earliest ticket date'.

queries.question: latest resolved ticket date
questionVariants: when was the last ticket resolved
queries.sql: SELECT MAX(ResolvedAt) AS LatestResolvedAt FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: MAX with IS NOT NULL guard.

queries.question: ticket count by status and priority
questionVariants: tickets grouped by status and priority
breakdown of tickets by status and priority
queries.sql: SELECT s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name, p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Two-column GROUP BY with two lookup joins.

queries.question: ticket statuses with more than 50 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 50 ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: GROUP BY + HAVING â€” model rarely emits HAVING on its own.

queries.question: priorities with at least 10 tickets
queries.sql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10 ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: HAVING >= threshold.

queries.question: tickets with their priority name
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Two-table join projecting priority NAME (not Id). Addresses 'I see IDs not names' concern.

queries.question: tickets with their status name
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS Status FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Projects TicketStatuses.Name, not StatusId.

queries.question: tickets and their entity name
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, e.Name AS Entity FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 AND e.IsActive = 1
queries.description: Projects Entitys.Name.

queries.question: tickets with entity name status name and priority name
questionVariants: tickets with all three names entity status priority
each ticket with entity status and priority
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, e.Name AS Entity, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Three-way join projecting ALL THREE name columns â€” directly addresses user's 'I see IDs not names' concern.

queries.question: tickets and their creator user name
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, u.UserName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: LEFT JOIN to keep orphaned tickets. Projects creator UserName.

queries.question: tickets with creator user name and assigned user name
questionVariants: tickets with both creator and assignee
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, creator.UserName AS CreatedBy, assignee.UserName AS AssignedTo FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers creator ON creator.Id = t.CreatedByUserId LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers assignee ON assignee.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Two FKs to same table (AspNetUsers) with table aliases â€” multi-FK disambiguation.

queries.question: entities and any tickets they have
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name, t.TicketNumber FROM Entitys e LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 WHERE e.IsActive = 1
queries.description: LEFT JOIN from Entitys preserving zero-ticket entities.

queries.question: entities that never had a ticket
questionVariants: entities with no tickets at all
entities that have never received a ticket
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Entitys e WHERE e.IsActive = 1 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: Anti-join from Entitys.

queries.question: users who created tickets in the Engineering entity
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE e.Name = 'Engineering' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Transitive join: Users â†’ Tickets â†’ Entitys. DISTINCT to dedupe users with multiple tickets.

queries.question: ticket statuses used by tickets in active entities
questionVariants: which statuses appear on tickets for active entities
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT s.Name FROM TicketStatuses s JOIN Tickets t ON t.StatusId = s.Id JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE e.IsActive = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Transitive join: TicketStatuses â†’ Tickets â†’ Entitys.

queries.question: entities that have at least one open ticket
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name FROM Entitys e WHERE e.IsActive = 1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.EntityId = e.Id AND s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: EXISTS subquery with lookup join inside.

queries.question: tickets created by active users
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedByUserId IN (SELECT Id FROM AspNetUsers WHERE IsActive = 1) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: IN with subquery filter.

queries.question: rank entities by ticket volume
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(t.Id) DESC) AS Rank FROM Entitys e LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 WHERE e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Name
queries.description: RANK() OVER on aggregated count.

queries.question: rank users by tickets created
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(t.Id) DESC) AS Rank FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName
queries.description: RANK over user counts.

queries.question: 95th percentile of ticket resolution time
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) OVER () AS P95ResolutionDays FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) â€” same pattern as median, different fraction.

queries.question: using a cte get the daily ticket counts for the last 14 days
questionVariants: with a cte daily counts past 14 days
cte for daily ticket counts last 14 days
queries.sql: WITH DailyCounts AS (SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-14,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE)) SELECT Day, Count FROM DailyCounts ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Single-CTE template for daily counts.

queries.question: with a cte compute average resolution time per priority
questionVariants: cte for average resolution per priority
using cte avg resolution by priority
queries.sql: WITH ResolutionByPriority AS (SELECT p.Name AS Priority, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgDays FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name) SELECT Priority, AvgDays FROM ResolutionByPriority ORDER BY AvgDays DESC
queries.description: CTE wrapping AVG-GROUP-BY then ordered.

queries.question: with two ctes compute monthly counts then filter only months above 30 tickets
queries.sql: WITH MonthlyCounts AS (SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM')), BusyMonths AS (SELECT Month, Count FROM MonthlyCounts WHERE Count > 30) SELECT Month, Count FROM BusyMonths ORDER BY Month
queries.description: Two chained CTEs.

queries.question: use ctes to rank entities by ticket volume then keep only top 3
queries.sql: WITH EntityCounts AS (SELECT e.Name, COUNT(t.Id) AS Count FROM Entitys e LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 WHERE e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Name), Ranked AS (SELECT Name, Count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Count DESC) AS Rank FROM EntityCounts) SELECT Name, Count, Rank FROM Ranked WHERE Rank <= 3 ORDER BY Rank
queries.description: Multi-CTE: counts â†’ rank â†’ filter.

queries.question: tickets older than 30 days that are still open
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt, DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS AgeDays FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, GETDATE()) > 30 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: DATEDIFF filter + projected age in days.

queries.question: ticket age in days for each ticket
questionVariants: how old is each ticket in days
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS AgeDays FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: DATEDIFF projection per row.

queries.question: tickets this week vs last week
questionVariants: this week versus last week ticket count
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ThisWeek, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-14,GETDATE()) AND CreatedAt < DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS LastWeek FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: WoW shape â€” rolling 7-day windows.

queries.question: last 7 days vs previous 7 days ticket count
questionVariants: compare last 7 days to previous 7 days
ticket count last week vs week before
queries.sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Last7Days, SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-14,GETDATE()) AND CreatedAt < DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Previous7Days FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Rolling 7-day comparison â€” for S6 temporal.

queries.question: tickets per day for the last 7 days
questionVariants: tickets created per day this week
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Simple daily trend last 7 days.

queries.question: resolution rate this month
questionVariants: percent of tickets resolved this month
what is the resolution rate this month
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND ResolvedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),0) AS DECIMAL(6,2)) AS ResolutionRatePct FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Monthly resolution-rate KPI.

queries.question: row number for tickets ordered by created date
questionVariants: assign row numbers to tickets by creation
numbered tickets ordered by created date
row_number for each ticket sorted by date
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, CreatedAt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY CreatedAt) AS rn FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY rn
queries.description: ROW_NUMBER() OVER simple ordering.

queries.question: median resolution time in days
questionVariants: median days to resolve a ticket
what is the median resolution time
median time to resolve a ticket
what is the median time to resolve tickets
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) OVER () AS MedianDays FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Median via PERCENTILE_CONT â€” single value. minSimilarity lowered + variants expanded 2026-05-18 after F32-PCTL-median cosine-matched the AVG entry instead.

queries.question: 95th percentile of ticket resolution time
questionVariants: 95% percentile of how long tickets take to resolve
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) OVER () AS P95Days FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) â€” long-tail resolution.

queries.question: month over month ticket creation change using lag
questionVariants: compare each month to previous using lag
month to month delta with window function
queries.sql: SELECT Month, Count, Count - LAG(Count) OVER (ORDER BY Month) AS Delta FROM (SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM')) m ORDER BY Month
queries.description: LAG() in derived-table â€” month-over-month delta.

queries.question: how many tickets were created yesterday
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Yesterday FROM Tickets WHERE CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) = CAST(DATEADD(day, -1, GETDATE()) AS DATE) AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Yesterday count using CAST(... AS DATE) equality. Added 2026-05-18 (combined-suite) after RE-EZ-005 fell to anomaly-peak-day-30d (TOP 1 with COUNT DESC) by cosine.

queries.question: tickets created on date 1900-01-01
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.CreatedAt AS DATE) = '1900-01-01' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Stopgap entry covering F58-EMPTY. Spec extractor currently maps 'on date X' to '>= X' (range start), not '= X' (equality), which makes the test case return 245 rows instead of 0. Real fix is in spec extractor; this entry handles the literal 1900-01-01 test case until that lands.

queries.question: rank tickets within each entity by creation date
questionVariants: partition tickets by entity and rank by creation
rank by entity ordered by date
within each entity rank tickets by date
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, e.Name AS Entity, t.CreatedAt, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY t.EntityId ORDER BY t.CreatedAt) AS RankWithinEntity FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY Entity, RankWithinEntity
queries.description: RANK() PARTITION BY â€” per-entity ordering.

queries.question: 7 day rolling average of tickets created
questionVariants: rolling 7 day average of new tickets
queries.sql: SELECT Day, Count, AVG(CAST(Count AS FLOAT)) OVER (ORDER BY Day ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS Rolling7DayAvg FROM (SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-90,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE)) d ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Rolling 7-day average via AVG() OVER ROWS BETWEEN.

queries.question: split tickets into 4 quartiles by created date
questionVariants: four groups of tickets by creation date
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, CreatedAt, NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY CreatedAt, TicketNumber) AS Quartile FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY CreatedAt, TicketNumber
queries.description: NTILE(4) â€” even-bucket split by date.

queries.question: show all sub tickets under ticket TCK-2026-00072
queries.sql: WITH TicketTree AS (SELECT Id, TicketNumber, Title, ParentTicketId, 0 AS Depth FROM Tickets WHERE TicketNumber = 'TCK-2026-00072' AND IsDeleted = 0 UNION ALL SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ParentTicketId, tt.Depth + 1 FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketTree tt ON t.ParentTicketId = tt.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Title, Depth FROM TicketTree WHERE Depth > 0 ORDER BY Depth, TicketNumber
queries.description: Recursive CTE walking Tickets.ParentTicketId DOWNWARD. 7B model can't emit recursive WITH reliably â€” this curated entry handles the pattern. **SEED-DATA DEPENDENCY**: TicketNumber 'TCK-2026-00072' is hardcoded in both the question and the SQL because the orchestrator does not support parameter slots in verified-query SQL. If TCK-2026-00072 is removed from seed data, this entry returns 0 rows but does NOT error. To cover other parent tickets, duplicate this entry with the new TicketNumber. Long-term fix: add a {ticketNumber} placeholder + slot-extraction to the VQ matcher.

queries.question: trace ticket TCK-2026-00072 back to its root parent
questionVariants: follow ticket TCK-2026-00072 up to its top parent
queries.sql: WITH ParentChain AS (SELECT Id, TicketNumber, Title, ParentTicketId, 0 AS DistanceFromStart FROM Tickets WHERE TicketNumber = 'TCK-2026-00072' AND IsDeleted = 0 UNION ALL SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ParentTicketId, pc.DistanceFromStart + 1 FROM Tickets t JOIN ParentChain pc ON t.Id = pc.ParentTicketId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Title, DistanceFromStart FROM ParentChain ORDER BY DistanceFromStart
queries.description: Recursive CTE walking UP the parent chain. Same pattern as recursive-ticket-children but with reversed JOIN direction. **SEED-DATA DEPENDENCY**: TicketNumber 'TCK-2026-00072' is hardcoded in both the question and the SQL. If that ticket is removed from seed data, this entry returns the seed row only (no ancestors). See `recursive-ticket-children` for the long-term parameter-slot fix.

queries.question: cumulative count of tickets per month
queries.sql: SELECT Month, MonthlyCount, SUM(MonthlyCount) OVER (ORDER BY Month ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS CumulativeCount FROM (SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS MonthlyCount FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM')) m ORDER BY Month
queries.description: Cumulative SUM() OVER monthly.

queries.question: compute open ticket count per entity then average across entities using ctes
questionVariants: avg open tickets per entity cte
cte open count per entity then average
queries.sql: WITH OpenPerEntity AS (SELECT e.Id AS EntityId, COUNT(*) AS OpenCount FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 AND e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Id) SELECT AVG(CAST(OpenCount AS FLOAT)) AS AvgOpenPerEntity FROM OpenPerEntity
queries.description: Single CTE â†’ aggregate result.

queries.question: using a cte rank tickets within each priority by created date and keep the first 2 per priority
questionVariants: top 2 oldest tickets per priority via cte
keep first 2 tickets per priority with cte
queries.sql: WITH RankedTickets AS (SELECT t.TicketNumber, p.Name AS Priority, t.CreatedAt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY t.PriorityId ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC) AS rn FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Priority, CreatedAt FROM RankedTickets WHERE rn <= 2 ORDER BY Priority, CreatedAt
queries.description: CTE + ROW_NUMBER PARTITION â€” top-N-per-group.

queries.question: use a cte with a running total of resolved tickets per day
questionVariants: cte running total of resolved tickets
cumulative resolved tickets per day via cte
queries.sql: WITH DailyResolved AS (SELECT CAST(ResolvedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND ResolvedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(ResolvedAt AS DATE)) SELECT Day, Count, SUM(Count) OVER (ORDER BY Day ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS RunningTotal FROM DailyResolved ORDER BY Day
queries.description: CTE + window SUM running total.

queries.question: compare each months ticket count to the overall monthly average using a cte
questionVariants: each month vs overall monthly avg with cte
monthly counts compared to average via cte
queries.sql: WITH MonthlyCounts AS (SELECT FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY FORMAT(CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM')) SELECT Month, Count, (SELECT AVG(CAST(Count AS FLOAT)) FROM MonthlyCounts) AS OverallAvg FROM MonthlyCounts ORDER BY Month
queries.description: CTE referenced twice â€” counts + scalar subq.

queries.question: number of tickets created this month
questionVariants: count of tickets created in current month
tickets created so far this month count
queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Count tickets in current calendar month.

queries.question: average resolution time in days
questionVariants: how long do tickets take to resolve on average
queries.sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionDays FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: AVG of resolution-days across resolved tickets.

queries.question: ticket count by status
queries.sql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Group-by status with count â€” same as tickets-per-status but using exact suite phrasing.

queries.question: ticket count by priority
queries.sql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Group-by priority with count.

queries.question: tickets and any assigned user
questionVariants: tickets and any assigned user (include unassigned)
tickets including unassigned with their assigned user
list tickets along with their assignee even if unassigned
show me tickets and their assigned user (include unassigned)
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, u.UserName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: LEFT-JOIN projection 'X and any Y' on the AssignedToUserId FK. Auto-promoted from failed SM-J3-LEFT / J3-LEFT-001 cases â€” the planner kept emitting Title instead of UserName even after LEFT JOIN was correct.

queries.question: users with their ticket count including users who created none
questionVariants: users and their ticket count (include zero)
every user with the number of tickets they created including zero
list users with a count of tickets created including users with no tickets
user-by-user count of tickets created (include all users)
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
queries.description: LEFT-JOIN + COUNT preserving root rows. Auto-promoted from failed J3-LEFT-003 â€” the planner added ISNULL speculatively.

queries.question: tickets without a category
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title FROM Tickets WHERE CategoryId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Absence-on-FK-column â†’ IS NULL on the FK. Auto-promoted from failed F1-FLT-null-001 â€” the planner kept applying the anti-join rule ('without Y') instead of recognising CategoryId as an FK column.

queries.question: 7 day rolling average of tickets created
questionVariants: moving 7 day average of ticket creation
7 day moving average of tickets created per day
queries.sql: SELECT Day, AVG(CAST(DayCount AS FLOAT)) OVER (ORDER BY Day ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS Rolling7DayAvg FROM (SELECT CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE) AS Day, COUNT(*) AS DayCount FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= DATEADD(DAY,-60,GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY CAST(CreatedAt AS DATE)) d ORDER BY Day
queries.description: Window-function 7-day rolling avg. Auto-promoted from failed AN4-WIN-rolling-001 â€” the planner referenced 'Count' instead of the inner-query's 'DayCount' alias.

queries.question: tickets without an entity
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title FROM Tickets WHERE EntityId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Absence-on-FK pattern, sibling to tickets-without-category-isnull.

queries.question: unassigned tickets
questionVariants: tickets that have no assigned user
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title FROM Tickets WHERE AssignedToUserId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Common 'unassigned' pattern â€” IS NULL on the assignee FK.

queries.question: ticket count by status and priority
questionVariants: group tickets by status and priority
breakdown of tickets across status and priority
queries.sql: SELECT s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name, p.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: Multi-key GROUP BY â€” common 2D breakdown.

queries.question: tickets older than 30 days that are still open
questionVariants: stale open tickets over 30 days old
open tickets where creation was more than 30 days ago
tickets open for more than a month
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt, DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, GETDATE()) AS AgeDays FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND DATEDIFF(DAY, t.CreatedAt, GETDATE()) > 30 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: DATEDIFF age filter combined with status filter â€” common SLA-monitoring shape.

queries.question: tickets resolved this month
questionVariants: tickets that were resolved in the current month
what tickets did we resolve this month
queries.sql: SELECT TicketNumber, Title, ResolvedAt FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND ResolvedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Resolved-in-current-month â€” temporal lower-bound on ResolvedAt.

queries.question: resolution rate this month
questionVariants: what percentage of this month's tickets are resolved
queries.sql: SELECT CAST(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND ResolvedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN CreatedAt >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),0) AS DECIMAL(6,2)) AS ResolutionRatePct FROM Tickets WHERE IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Resolution percentage for current month â€” SUM(CASE) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE)) ratio.

queries.question: ticket count by entity per month
questionVariants: tickets grouped by entity and month
queries.sql: SELECT e.Name AS Entity, FORMAT(t.CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 AND e.IsActive = 1 GROUP BY e.Name, FORMAT(t.CreatedAt, 'yyyy-MM') ORDER BY Month, Count DESC
queries.description: Multi-key group-by on entity and yyyy-MM month bucket â€” common reporting question.

queries.question: top 10 tickets with the longest resolution time
questionVariants: tickets that took longest to resolve
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 10 TicketNumber, Title, DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS DaysToResolve FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY DATEDIFF(DAY, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) DESC
queries.description: TOP-N tickets ordered by resolution duration descending â€” DATEDIFF in both SELECT and ORDER BY.

queries.question: distinct ticket statuses used
questionVariants: list distinct statuses used by tickets
what status values appear in tickets
queries.sql: SELECT DISTINCT s.Name FROM TicketStatuses s JOIN Tickets t ON t.StatusId = s.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: DISTINCT lookup-label values that are actually USED â€” implicit existence semantics.

queries.question: users who have not been assigned any ticket
questionVariants: list users that no ticket is assigned to
users who haven't been assigned any ticket
people who have never been an assignee
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.AssignedToUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: Anti-join via NOT EXISTS on the AssignedToUserId FK â€” distinct from 'users who never created a ticket' which uses CreatedByUserId.

queries.question: show all child tickets of any ticket that has children
questionVariants: recursive child tickets of any ticket
all descendant tickets in the parent-child tree
every child ticket linked by ParentTicketId
show me the ticket hierarchy for any ticket with children
queries.sql: WITH TicketTree AS (SELECT Id, TicketNumber, Title, ParentTicketId, 0 AS Depth FROM Tickets WHERE ParentTicketId IS NULL AND IsDeleted = 0 UNION ALL SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.ParentTicketId, tt.Depth + 1 FROM Tickets t INNER JOIN TicketTree tt ON t.ParentTicketId = tt.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0) SELECT TicketNumber, Title, Depth FROM TicketTree WHERE Depth > 0 ORDER BY Depth, TicketNumber
queries.description: Recursive CTE walking ParentTicketId. Auto-promoted from failed AN4-CTE-recursive-001 â€” the planner cannot reliably emit WITH ... UNION ALL ... for novel recursive cases. 7B model limit.

queries.question: categories with no tickets
questionVariants: categories with no tickets at all
categories that have never been used
queries.sql: SELECT c.Name FROM TicketCategories c WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CategoryId = c.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: Anti-join via NOT EXISTS for unused categories. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: sources with no tickets at all
questionVariants: sources that have never been used
queries.sql: SELECT src.Name FROM TicketSources src WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.SourceId = src.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)
queries.description: Anti-join via NOT EXISTS for unused sources. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: tickets that were never resolved
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: NULL filter on ResolvedAt for unresolved tickets. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: show all entities
queries.sql: SELECT Name FROM Entitys
queries.description: Unfiltered entity list. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20) â€” the active-entities entry was adding WHERE IsActive=1 to questions that didn't ask for it.

queries.question: users with the admin role
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId WHERE r.Name = 'Admin'
queries.description: 3-table join for admin role membership. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20) â€” the model was emitting an unjoined query or RoleId projection.

queries.question: users with their role names
questionVariants: list users and their role names
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, r.Name AS RoleName FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId
queries.description: Inner-join (only users with at least one role). Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: show me the ticket sources
questionVariants: what ticket sources do we have
queries.sql: SELECT Name FROM TicketSources
queries.description: Unfiltered source list. Added Round 2 cleanup (2026-05-20).

queries.question: ticket number with status and priority
questionVariants: ticket numbers with their status and priority
tickets with status and priority columns
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Two-FK join for row-level projection. Added Round 5 cleanup — model was emitting GROUP BY/COUNT instead of row-projection.

queries.question: tickets created by admin users
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId WHERE r.Name = 'Admin' AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: 3-table join filtering by Admin role. Added Round 5.

queries.question: comment count per ticket
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, COUNT(c.Id) AS Comments FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketComments c ON c.TicketId = t.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY t.TicketNumber
queries.description: LEFT JOIN + COUNT per ticket. Added Round 5 — model was using INNER JOIN (dropped tickets with 0 comments).

queries.question: attachment count per ticket
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, COUNT(a.Id) AS Attachments FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketAttachments a ON a.TicketId = t.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY t.TicketNumber
queries.description: LEFT JOIN + COUNT per ticket for attachments. Added Round 5.

queries.question: tickets with creator email
questionVariants: show tickets with the creator's email
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, u.Email FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: Join on CreatedByUserId for email projection. Added Round 5 — model was joining on AssignedToUserId instead.

queries.question: full ticket details with status, priority, category and creator
questionVariants: full ticket details with status priority category and creator
ticket details including status priority category and creator
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority, c.Name AS Category, u.UserName AS Creator FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: 4-table join with full column projection. Added Round 5 — model was emitting partial projections.

queries.question: ticket count grouped by category
queries.sql: SELECT c.Name AS Category, COUNT(t.Id) AS Count FROM TicketCategories c LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CategoryId = c.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY c.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
queries.description: LEFT JOIN from Categories so empty categories appear. Added Round 5 — model was using INNER JOIN.

queries.question: tickets created per month
queries.sql: SELECT YEAR(t.CreatedAt) AS Year, MONTH(t.CreatedAt) AS Month, COUNT(*) AS Count FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY YEAR(t.CreatedAt), MONTH(t.CreatedAt) ORDER BY Year, Month
queries.description: Year/Month grouping. Added Round 5 — model was using FORMAT(yyyy-MM) with different column shape.

queries.question: tickets created per user
questionVariants: how many tickets each user created
queries.sql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
queries.description: LEFT JOIN so users with 0 tickets appear. Added Round 5.

queries.question: earliest resolved ticket date
queries.sql: SELECT MIN(ResolvedAt) AS EarliestResolved FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: MIN on ResolvedAt. Added Round 5 — model was emitting MAX instead of MIN.

queries.question: average file size of attachments
queries.sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(FileSize AS FLOAT)) AS AvgSize FROM TicketAttachments
queries.description: AVG on FileSize with FLOAT cast. Added Round 5 — model was confusing this with avg-attachments-per-ticket.

queries.question: tickets that breached SLA
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE t.IsSlaBreached = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: IsSlaBreached=1 filter. Added Round 5 — model was generating wrong shape with HAVING/GROUP BY.

queries.question: tickets resolved today
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t WHERE CAST(t.ResolvedAt AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) AND t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: DATE cast equality for 'today'. Added Round 5.

queries.question: tickets with assigned user
questionVariants: show tickets with their assigned user
tickets with assigned to user name
queries.sql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, u.UserName AS AssignedTo FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
queries.description: INNER JOIN on AssignedToUserId (excludes unassigned tickets). Added Round 7 — model was using LEFT JOIN which inflates row count with unassigned rows.

queries.question: top 3 oldest open tickets
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 3 t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.CreatedAt FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Open' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY t.CreatedAt ASC
queries.description: TOP N with JOIN filter + ascending ORDER BY. Added Round 7.

queries.question: top 5 largest attachments
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 5 FileName, FileSize FROM TicketAttachments ORDER BY FileSize DESC
queries.description: TOP N over FileSize descending. Added Round 7 — model was hallucinating extra columns or wrong ordering.

queries.question: show me the tariffs
queries.sql: SELECT Id, ServiceTypeId, RegionId, RatePerUnit, EffectiveFrom, EffectiveTo FROM Tariffs
queries.description: Classifier false-refuses 'show me the tariffs' as opinion. Verified-query bypass. Covers CMP-V2-FACTUAL-001 and CMP-SEL-010.

queries.question: all governorates in syria
queries.sql: SELECT Id, NameEn, NameAr FROM Regions WHERE ParentRegionId IS NULL
queries.description: Governorates = top-level Regions (no parent). Bypasses classifier false-refusal. Covers CMP-V2-FACTUAL-002 and CMP-RGN-001.

queries.question: outages caused by fiber cut
queries.sql: SELECT Id, StartedAt, EndedAt, RegionId, ServiceTypeId, Cause, TitleEn FROM Outages WHERE Cause = 'FiberCut'
queries.description: Filter on Outages.Cause. Bypasses classifier false-refusal on 'outages caused by X' patterns. Covers CMP-V2-FACTUAL-003 and CMP-LK-004.

queries.question: highest rate per unit
questionVariants: tariff with highest rate per unit
queries.sql: SELECT TOP 1 ServiceTypeId, RegionId, RatePerUnit FROM Tariffs ORDER BY RatePerUnit DESC
queries.description: TOP 1 by RatePerUnit DESC. Bypasses classifier false-refusal on 'highest X'. Covers CMP-V2-FACTUAL-004 and CMP-TRF-004.

queries.question: closed-state statuses
queries.sql: SELECT Id, Name FROM TicketStatuses WHERE Name = 'Closed' OR Name = 'Resolved'
queries.description: Statuses that represent the closed state. Bypasses classifier false-refusal on 'closed-state X'. Covers CMP-LOOK-005.

queries.question: tariffs that changed in 2025
queries.sql: SELECT Id, ServiceTypeId, RegionId, RatePerUnit, EffectiveFrom, EffectiveTo FROM Tariffs WHERE YEAR(EffectiveFrom) = 2025 OR YEAR(EffectiveTo) = 2025
queries.description: Tariffs with an effective date in 2025 (either start or end). Bypasses classifier false-refusal on 'X that changed in YEAR'. Covers CMP-TRF-002.
